parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff spans 6,600+ lines across 84 files. Without access to inspect the actual file contents (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, glm-client.mjs, state.mjs, etc.), I cannot confirm absence of defects in the core runtime paths. Two structural risks are defensible from the context alone: (1) scripts/lib/process.mjs was deleted entirely, and any surviving transitive import will cause a deterministic runtime crash; (2) the new retry.mjs (203 lines) and bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) modules introduce retry/dispatch logic that, if it silently swallows errors or retries non-idempotent operations, will produce hard-to-diagnose silent failures under real API degradation. The diff is too large and touches too many runtime-critical paths to approve without line-level inspection.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs may leave dangling imports causing runtime crash
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines removed) is shown as fully deleted in the diff stat. If any other module—glm-companion.mjs, glm-client.mjs, job-control.mjs, or any test file—still contains a static import of this module, Node.js will throw MODULE_NOT_FOUND at startup. This class of regression is easy to miss in a 6,600-line diff and would manifest as a hard crash on every invocation, not a degraded-path error. The diff does not include a grep or CI check confirming zero remaining references to 'process.mjs' or 'lib/process'.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/ tests/` to confirm zero surviving references. If any exist, either restore the file with a re-export shim or update the import sites to use whatever replaced it.

findings.title: New retry.mjs (203 lines) introduces retry logic without visible idempotency guards
findings.body: A 203-line retry module is a significant addition. Retry logic is a classic source of silent data duplication (retrying a non-idempotent POST/mutation), infinite loops (buggy backoff that never reaches maxAttempts), or error swallowing (catching and retrying errors that indicate permanent failure). Without inspecting the actual implementation I cannot confirm these are handled, and the diff stat alone shows no corresponding integration tests that would exercise retry-under-failure against the real glm-client API surface. The existing retry.test.mjs (352 lines) suggests unit coverage exists, but unit tests typically mock the failure mode rather than proving idempotency against real side effects.
findings.recommendation: Inspect retry.mjs to confirm: (1) it never retries non-idempotent operations without explicit opt-in, (2) maxAttempts is enforced as a hard ceiling, (3) permanent-failure error codes are not retried, (4) there is jitter on backoff to prevent thundering herds. Add a comment or test explicitly documenting which glm-client call sites use retry and whether they are idempotent.

findings.title: 6,600-line cumulative diff with no v0.4.7 tag makes regression surface unbounded
findings.body: The user focus references 'v0.4.7 sampling-sweep run 76' but no v0.4.7 tag exists in the commit log—the latest is v0.4.6. The diff from v0.4.0 to HEAD spans 84 files and 6,600+ insertions. This means: (a) the exact code under review is ambiguous (is it v0.4.6 HEAD, or uncommitted changes?), and (b) the regression surface is too large for any reviewer to confidently enumerate failure modes across retry, error dispatch, client, state, job-control, and render modules simultaneously. Point releases (0.4.x) carrying this magnitude of change violate the principle of minimal blast radius.
findings.recommendation: Clarify exactly which commit is under review. If shipping v0.4.7, ensure the tag is cut and the diff is pinned. For future releases, consider batching changes into smaller, independently testable increments rather than a single 6,600-line cumulative release.

next_steps: Run `grep -rn 'lib/process' scripts/ tests/` to confirm the deleted process.mjs has zero surviving imports.
Inspect retry.mjs for idempotency assumptions—specifically which glm-client methods are retried and whether they have side effects.
Inspect bigmodel-errors.mjs to verify error classification does not swallow unexpected error shapes (e.g., network timeouts returning non-standard error bodies).
Pin the exact commit under review and cut the v0.4.7 tag before proceeding with the sampling-sweep run.
Run the existing test suite (`node --test tests/*.test.mjs`) against the target commit and confirm zero failures before shipping.
